---
title: "Emiliano Ariel Videla Rodriguez"
subtitle: "Teaching Co-ordinator"
description: |
  All I know is that I know nothing
image: EAVR.jpg
# date: UPDATE
author: 
  - name: Teaching Co-ordinator
draft: false
---

<!-- upload your picture as a .jpg file in your corresponding folder and then add the name of the file in line 31 before the .jpg-->

![](EAVR.jpg){fig-align="center"}

<!--Write a few paragraph about yourself. Think about the message you want to give to the people who will be taking a look at your blog post. Make sure you provide the following pieces of information:  

 - Name: e.g., Emiliano Ariel Videla Rodriguez
 
 - Job: e.g., Teaching Co-ordinator
 
 - About Me/Bio: (3+ Sentences) "I am X who does Y, I care about X..."
 
 - Current roles: Lecturer in ABC, year 2 lead. quality committee member...
 
 - Areas of specialty: (technical/other thing someone is likely to want to talk to you about, or you feel proud of), as a list of bullet-points, each being 1-2 sentences. 
 
 - Courses I teach: list of course names, tag them as PG?UG and provide the corresponding links.
 
 - Projects and Resources: here link to non-course things you do, projects, organisations, each been a short sentence/words and provide the corresponding links. 
 
 - Publications, Talks: provide the list of articles/reports you have published. Follow a consistent citation style (Authors, Year, Title, Volume, Pages, et cetera). Include links to the publisher website. Alternatively, you could provide a link to google scholar. For example: EA Videla Rodriguez, JBO Mitchell, and VA Smith. 2024. Robust identification of interactions between heat-stress responsive genes in the chicken brain using Bayesian networks and augmented expression data. Scientific Reports. 14:9019. https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-58679-3. 
 
 - Timeline of Education and past jobs: here if you'd like, put your timeline as bullet-points. You can start with a year. 
 
 - Research and supervision: a small list of your most proud achievements. If you have things to share, especially if there are links. 
 
 - Things I do outside of work: if you'd like
 
 - Links and contact: email, github, linkedin, google scholar. 
 
To divide your blog into sections, you can use the # symbol to have clear headings (#Header 1; ##Header 2; ###Header 3). For example:
# References
## 2025

Paper 1
Paper 2

## 2024

Paper 1
Paper 2

-->
# Short Bio

Emiliano is a Teaching Co-ordinator. He did his PhD at the University of St. Andrews....

# Publication
## 2025

-  EA Videla Rodriguez. 2025. Bayesian....

-  EA Videla Rodriguez. 2025. An approach to....

<!--If you are working in RStudio, you can take a look at your blog by clicking the "Render" button (blue arrow pointing to the right)-->
